title: Course Schedule
hints: Model the courses and prerequisites as a directed graph where edges point from prerequisite to dependent course.
The problem reduces to detecting a cycle in this directed graph. If a cycle exists, you cannot finish all courses.

You can decide this constructively: repeatedly remove any course that currently has no outstanding prerequisites, which in turn frees others. If you manage to remove every course this way, there is no cycle; if you get stuck with courses remaining but none removable, a cycle is trapping them.
